subject: [PATCH] fuse: when copying a folio delay the mark dirty until the end

reviews.author: Darrick Wong
reviews.summary: Reviewer Darrick Wong noted that xfs can create large folios in its pagecache, which may interact negatively with the patch's extra folio_test_dirty() check.
reviews.raw_body:
/me stumbles in-thread to note that xfs gets large folios for its files'
pagecache fairly frequently now, especially as readahead ramps up.

Ok back to the hell that is deploying ClownStrike through a Java program
while Firefox repeatedly drives my laptop to OOM.

--D

2026-03-18.patch_summary: This patch delays marking a folio dirty until the end of a copy operation, potentially reducing overhead by avoiding unnecessary checks for page dirtiness.

reviews.author: Horst Birthelmer
reviews.summary: Reviewer Horst Birthelmer reported that he was unable to reproduce the issue described in the patch on passthrough_hp, and suggested that it may not be a problem even without large folios enabled.
sentiment_signals: reviewer's inability to reproduce the issue
suggestion that it may not be a problem
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Joanne,

I have tried to reproduce this with passthrough_hp and I never saw it.
So my answer would be something like: I don't think so.

This happens even with large folios disabled. I was just trying to
solve it, since I figured it will be worse with large folios.

Thanks,
Horst

2026-03-19.patch_summary: This patch delays marking a folio dirty until the end of a copy operation, potentially reducing overhead by avoiding unnecessary checks for page dirtiness.
